trans-unit.source: Exercise 7: Reachability of health Posts from CRM Warehouses
trans-unit.target: Exercise 7: Reachability of health Posts from CRM Warehouses

trans-unit.source: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.
trans-unit.target: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.

trans-unit.source: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.
trans-unit.target: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.

trans-unit.source: This exercise is part of the [Madagascar Anticipatory Action Cyclon Analysis Exercise Track](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Exercise_tracks/en_mdg_aa_cyclones.html)
trans-unit.target: This exercise is part of the [Madagascar Anticipatory Action Cyclon Analysis Exercise Track](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Exercise_tracks/en_mdg_aa_cyclones.html)

trans-unit.source: :::{card} :class-card: sd-border-1 sd-shadow-none __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^ Aina, the GIS expert at the Malagasy Red Cross (CRM), is preparing for the upcoming cyclone season. She wants to improve her team’s ability to act quickly once a storm is forecasted by automating key analyses in QGIS. These include estimating exposed populations, identifying impacted services like health and education, and assessing whether health posts can be reached from key warehouses within a critical 10-hour window. The goal is to prepare an end-to-end analysis and visualization workflow that can support fast, data-driven anticipatory action before a cyclone makes landfall.
trans-unit.target: :::{card} :class-card: sd-border-1 sd-shadow-none __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^ Aina, the GIS expert at the Malagasy Red Cross (CRM), is preparing for the upcoming cyclone season. She wants to improve her team’s ability to act quickly once a storm is forecasted by automating key analyses in QGIS. These include estimating exposed populations, identifying impacted services like health and education, and assessing whether health posts can be reached from key warehouses within a critical 10-hour window. The goal is to prepare an end-to-end analysis and visualization workflow that can support fast, data-driven anticipatory action before a cyclone makes landfall.

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on training conduction
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on training conduction

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: When a cyclone is forecast to make landfall, Aina works with the logistics and health teams to decide **where to send prepositioned medical kits**. However, not all CRM warehouses stock the needed items — only three do.
trans-unit.target: When a cyclone is forecast to make landfall, Aina works with the logistics and health teams to decide **where to send prepositioned medical kits**. However, not all CRM warehouses stock the needed items — only three do.

trans-unit.source: To make fast, data-driven decisions, Aina wants to know **which health posts are reachable** from those warehouses **within 10 hours**. This analysis helps ensure that kits are sent to facilities **that can actually be reached in time**.
trans-unit.target: To make fast, data-driven decisions, Aina wants to know **which health posts are reachable** from those warehouses **within 10 hours**. This analysis helps ensure that kits are sent to facilities **that can actually be reached in time**.

trans-unit.source: Her goal is to create a clear visual map showing reachable vs. non-reachable health posts — and share this with decision-makers as quickly as possible.
trans-unit.target: Her goal is to create a clear visual map showing reachable vs. non-reachable health posts — and share this with decision-makers as quickly as possible.

trans-unit.source: 1. Filter Health Posts from the National Health Facility Dataset
trans-unit.target: 1. Filter Health Posts from the National Health Facility Dataset

trans-unit.source: Before checking which facilities are reachable, Aina needs to isolate **health posts** from the broader dataset of all health facilities.
trans-unit.target: Before checking which facilities are reachable, Aina needs to isolate **health posts** from the broader dataset of all health facilities.

trans-unit.source: File: `hotosm_mdg_health_facilities_points.gpkg` (or the respective GeoPackage you are using)
trans-unit.target: File: `hotosm_mdg_health_facilities_points.gpkg` (or the respective GeoPackage you are using)

trans-unit.source: Load it via drag and drop or through `Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`.
trans-unit.target: Load it via drag and drop or through `Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`.

trans-unit.source: **Open the attribute table** and check the column named `amenity`.
trans-unit.target: **Open the attribute table** and check the column named `amenity`.

trans-unit.source: **Filter by expression** to keep only health posts:
trans-unit.target: **Filter by expression** to keep only health posts:

trans-unit.source: Right-click the filtered layer in the Layers Panel → `Export` → `Save Features As…`
trans-unit.target: Right-click the filtered layer in the Layers Panel → `Export` → `Save Features As…`

trans-unit.source: **Remove the filter** or original layer from your project to avoid confusion.
trans-unit.target: **Remove the filter** or original layer from your project to avoid confusion.

trans-unit.source: 💡 **Tip**: Filtering directly in QGIS lets you work with a specific subset of features without modifying the original dataset.
trans-unit.target: 💡 **Tip**: Filtering directly in QGIS lets you work with a specific subset of features without modifying the original dataset.

trans-unit.source: 2. Load Isochrone Layers for the Three CRM Warehouses
trans-unit.target: 2. Load Isochrone Layers for the Three CRM Warehouses

trans-unit.source: Aina knows that only **three warehouses** stock the necessary medical supplies: **Antananarivo**, **Maroantsetra**, and **Tolanaro**. She will now load the isochrone layers for each of these warehouses to begin analyzing service areas.
trans-unit.target: Aina knows that only **three warehouses** stock the necessary medical supplies: **Antananarivo**, **Maroantsetra**, and **Tolanaro**. She will now load the isochrone layers for each of these warehouses to begin analyzing service areas.

trans-unit.source: **Load the individual isochrone layers** for each warehouse:
trans-unit.target: **Load the individual isochrone layers** for each warehouse:

trans-unit.source: You can drag and drop each file into QGIS or go to `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`.
trans-unit.target: You can drag and drop each file into QGIS or go to `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add Vector Layer`.

trans-unit.source: **Inspect the attribute table** of each isochrone layer Confirm that each record has a `traveltime_h` field showing the estimated travel time in **hours**.
trans-unit.target: **Inspect the attribute table** of each isochrone layer Confirm that each record has a `traveltime_h` field showing the estimated travel time in **hours**.

trans-unit.source: **Remove all features where travel time is above 10 hours**:
trans-unit.target: **Remove all features where travel time is above 10 hours**:

trans-unit.source: **Export each filtered layer** to the `temp` folder : At this point, Aina also ensures all exported layers are saved in the same CRS as the health post dataset — `EPSG:4326` — to avoid problems in the spatial join.
trans-unit.target: **Export each filtered layer** to the `temp` folder : At this point, Aina also ensures all exported layers are saved in the same CRS as the health post dataset — `EPSG:4326` — to avoid problems in the spatial join.

trans-unit.source: **Style the isochrones for clarity** Aina can apply predefined style file to color the layer based on `traveltime_h` to visualize different time bands (4h, 6h, 8h, 10h) later in Step 5.
trans-unit.target: **Style the isochrones for clarity** Aina can apply predefined style file to color the layer based on `traveltime_h` to visualize different time bands (4h, 6h, 8h, 10h) later in Step 5.

trans-unit.source: 3. Visualizing Health Post Reachability from CRM Warehouses
trans-unit.target: 3. Visualizing Health Post Reachability from CRM Warehouses

trans-unit.source: Aina needs to identify which health posts can be reached by road from three key CRM warehouses (Antananarivo, Maroantsetra, and Tolanaro) **within 10 hours of travel time**. She will do this manually by combining the 10-hour isochrones from these warehouses and comparing them to the national health post dataset.
trans-unit.target: Aina needs to identify which health posts can be reached by road from three key CRM warehouses (Antananarivo, Maroantsetra, and Tolanaro) **within 10 hours of travel time**. She will do this manually by combining the 10-hour isochrones from these warehouses and comparing them to the national health post dataset.

trans-unit.source: **Merge the Isochrone Layers from the Three Warehouses**
trans-unit.target: **Merge the Isochrone Layers from the Three Warehouses**

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Merge Vector Layers`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Merge Vector Layers`.

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Select by Location`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Select by Location`.

trans-unit.source: 💡 The selected points are those within the 10-hour service areas of the warehouses.
trans-unit.target: 💡 The selected points are those within the 10-hour service areas of the warehouses.

trans-unit.source: **Create a Reachability Field for Selected Health Posts**
trans-unit.target: **Create a Reachability Field for Selected Health Posts**

trans-unit.source: Open the **Field Calculator** ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) on the `health_posts_only` layer.
trans-unit.target: Open the **Field Calculator** ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) on the `health_posts_only` layer.

trans-unit.source: Click **OK** to create and populate the new field for selected features.
trans-unit.target: Click **OK** to create and populate the new field for selected features.

trans-unit.source: **Mark the Remaining Health Posts as Not Reachable**
trans-unit.target: **Mark the Remaining Health Posts as Not Reachable**

trans-unit.source: Invert the selection: Go to `Edit` → `Invert Feature Selection` ![](/fig/mActionInvertSelection.png) or right-click the layer and select `Invert Selection`.
trans-unit.target: Invert the selection: Go to `Edit` → `Invert Feature Selection` ![](/fig/mActionInvertSelection.png) or right-click the layer and select `Invert Selection`.

trans-unit.source: ✅ Now all health posts are labeled as either **reachable** or **not reachable** in the `Reachability_time` column.
trans-unit.target: ✅ Now all health posts are labeled as either **reachable** or **not reachable** in the `Reachability_time` column.
